pom.xml.general_idea: Add PMD JavaScript analyzer dependency to enable linting of JavaScript files.
Split PMD plugin configuration into separate executions for Java, JavaScript, and Tests with specific rulesets.
Add maven-javadoc-plugin to attach Javadoc JARs during the build.
Add maven-source-plugin to attach Source JARs during the build.
Add maven-surefire-plugin configuration to disable stack trace trimming and exclude integration tests.
Add ArchUnit JUnit5 engine dependency for architectural unit testing.
Add nexus-staging-maven-plugin to manage deployment to Sonatype Nexus.
Add a 'ci' profile to configure build behavior for continuous integration environments.
Add a 'pit' profile to enable mutation testing with Pitest.
Add an 'owasp' profile to enable dependency checking for security vulnerabilities.
Add a 'depgraph' profile to generate dependency graph visualizations.
Update RevAPI plugin configuration to check dependencies and fail on unresolved artifacts.
Remove explicit distribution management URLs, likely delegating to the nexus-staging plugin.

.github/dependabot.yml.general_idea: Configure Dependabot to use empty commit message prefixes for Maven, GitHub Actions, and NPM ecosystems.
Update the ignore list to exclude net.javacrumbs.json-unit:json-unit-assertj versions >= 3.0.0 instead of webjars dependencies.

.github/labels.yml.general_idea: Replace separate 'deprecated' and 'removed' labels with a single 'breaking' label to simplify issue tracking.
.github/labels.yml.human_impl:
- name: deprecated
- name: removed
+ name: breaking

.github/labels.yml.general_idea: Correct the spelling of 'description' in the 'java' label definition.
.github/labels.yml.human_impl:
- desccription: Pull requests that update Maven Java dependencies
+ description: Pull requests that update Maven Java dependencies

.github/labels.yml.general_idea: Correct the spelling of 'description' in the 'javascript' label definition.
.github/labels.yml.human_impl:
- desccription: Pull requests that update Node Javascript dependencies
+ description: Pull requests that update Node Javascript dependencies

.github/labels.yml.general_idea: Correct the spelling of 'description' in the 'github_actions' label definition.
.github/labels.yml.human_impl:
- desccription: Pull requests that update Github Actions code
+ description: Pull requests that update Github Actions workflows

.github/labels.yml.general_idea: Remove the specific year reference from the 'hacktoberfest' label description to make it evergreen.
.github/labels.yml.human_impl:
- description: Pull requests that participate in Hacktoberfest 2021
+ description: Pull requests that participate in Hacktoberfest

.github/release-drafter.yml.general_idea: Refactor release drafter configuration to use single labels instead of lists and rename categories for clarity.
.github/release-drafter.yml.human_impl: Changed 'labels' arrays to single 'label' strings and renamed 'Removed' to 'Breaking Changes'.

.github/workflows/assign-pr.yml.general_idea: Upgrade the auto-assign-action to version 2.0.0 to utilize newer features or bug fixes.
Rename the job identifier from 'add-reviews' to 'assign-pr' for better clarity.
Add an explicit name property to the job for better visibility in the GitHub Actions UI.

.github/workflows/ci.yml.general_idea: Remove the 'master' branch from the CI trigger list as it is being deprecated in favor of 'main'.
Upgrade the GitHub Actions checkout action from version 3 to version 4.
Upgrade the GitHub Actions setup-java action from version 3 to version 4.
Add a dedicated step to set up a specific version of Maven (3.9.9) using a community action.
.github/workflows/ci.yml.human_impl:
+      - name: Set up Maven
+        uses: stCarolas/setup-maven@v5
+        with:
+          maven-version: 3.9.9

.github/workflows/ci.yml.general_idea: Update the Maven build command to include color output and remove the explicit file path argument.
.github/workflows/ci.yml.human_impl:
-        run: mvn -V -ntp clean verify --file pom.xml '-Djenkins.test.timeout=5000' '-Dgpg.skip'
+        run: mvn -V --color always -ntp clean verify '-Djenkins.test.timeout=5000' '-Dgpg.skip'

.github/workflows/run-release-drafter.ym.general_idea: Bump the release-drafter GitHub Action from v5 to v6 to align with the project's migration to Java 17 and modernize the CI workflow.
Add 'master' to the list of branches that trigger the release drafter workflow to support repositories using the legacy default branch name.
Rename the workflow job ID and display name for consistency and clarity.
Simplify the top-level workflow name for brevity.

.github/workflows/sync-labels.yml.general_idea: Add 'master' branch to the push trigger to support repositories using the legacy default branch name.
Include the workflow file itself in the path triggers to allow the workflow to update itself.
Rename the job ID from 'build' to 'sync-labels' for better semantic clarity.
Add an explicit name property to the job for improved visibility in the GitHub Actions UI.
Update the checkout action from v3 to v4 to use the latest stable version.

.gitignore.general_idea: Ignore backup files to keep the repository clean.

LICENSE.general_idea: Update the copyright year range in the license file to reflect the current year.

README.md.general_idea: Remove the Maven Central badge from the README.
Update the MIT license badge link to point to Wikipedia instead of opensource.org.
Add a badge indicating the project targets JDK 17.
Fix the typo 'you own' to 'your own' in the description text.
README.md.human_impl:
-as parent of you own POM.
+as parent of your own POM.

README.md.general_idea: Update the Javadoc tool link to the current Oracle URL.
Reformat paragraph breaks and spacing in the README introduction for better readability.
README.md.human_impl:
-Each Java project should follow a given coding style. I.e., all contributions to the source code should use the same 
-formatting rules, design principles, code patterns, idioms, etc. I published such a coding style in my 
-[GitHub project 'codingstyle'](https://github.com/uhafner/codingstyle). I am using this coding style in my lectures about 
-software development at Munich University of Applied Sciences and in all of my open source projects.  
+Each Java project should follow a given coding style. 
+I.e., all contributions to the source code should use the same formatting rules, design principles, code patterns, idioms, etc. 
+I published such a coding style in my [GitHub project 'codingstyle'](https://github.com/uhafner/codingstyle). 
+I am using this coding style in my lectures about software development at Munich University of Applied Sciences and in all of my open source projects.
